prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_FT_catboost', seasonality_champion='ONLY_CWT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.8989) and high seasonality variance (0.7855), with early horizon specialists like THETA differing significantly from late horizon models like NaiveSeasonal. Tier1 models dominate overall RMSE while specialized trend-champion ONLY_FT_catboost and seasonality-champion ONLY_CWT_catboost show distinct strengths despite lower tier rankings, strongly supporting DBA combination as the optimal approach to leverage diverse model behaviors.

  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",

    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.8989,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,

  },
  "cot_narrative": "The analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.8989) and high seasonality variance (0.7855), with early horizon specialists like THETA differing significantly from late horizon models like NaiveSeasonal. Tier1 models dominate overall RMSE while specialized trend-champion ONLY_FT_catboost and seasonality-champion ONLY_CWT_catboost show distinct strengths despite lower tier rankings, strongly supporting DBA combination as the optimal approach to leverage diverse model behaviors."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "ridge_stacking_l250_topk3"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity, favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=0.8989 (high dispersion) supports DBA combination as recommended by pattern_analyst_insights. With n_windows=3 (few windows), small top_k=3 and shrinkage=0.25 provide stability. ridge_stacking_l250_topk3 adds meta-learning diversity. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures early horizon specialists like THETA (RMSE=577.8) while inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 balances tier1 models with shrinkage."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.8989) and high seasonality variance (0.7855), with early horizon specialists like THETA differing significantly from late horizon models like NaiveSeasonal. Tier1 models dominate overall RMSE while specialized trend-champion ONLY_FT_catboost and seasonality-champion ONLY_CWT_catboost show distinct strengths despite lower tier rankings, strongly supporting DBA combination as the optimal approach to leverage diverse model behaviors.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity, favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=0.8989 (high dispersion) supports DBA combination as recommended by pattern_analyst_insights. With n_windows=3 (few windows), small top_k=3 and shrinkage=0.25 provide stability. ridge_stacking_l250_topk3 adds meta-learning diversity. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures early horizon specialists like THETA (RMSE=577.8) while inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 balances tier1 models with shrinkage.
